description: MCPS-070 (MCP-RE-EPIC-P6.6C). The checked-in, machine-checkable map from each MCP-RE security property (P6.6A + P6.6B) to the Bazel test target AND the named test function that PROVES it. Docs reference this manifest ('see the manifest') instead of stale literal counts. Guarded by //mcp-re-conformance:security_traceability_guard_test (MCPS-070): every entry's bazel_target must be declared by a real BUILD.bazel rule and every test_fn must appear in the referenced test source on disk. A renamed or removed target/fn makes that guard FAIL. Each entry's source is read from DISK at test time via runfiles (the same $(rlocationpath) scheme as the conformance drift_guard); nothing is trusted as written.

entries.property: mTLS client/server happy path: trusted server + client round-trip succeeds
entries.notes: mTLS transport happy path: trusted server cert + trusted client cert complete a mutual-auth round trip.

entries.property: server-cert verification: untrusted server cert REJECTED by the client
entries.notes: Server-auth case 1 of 4. A server cert from a CA the client does not trust is rejected during the handshake.

entries.property: server-cert verification: trusted server cert ACCEPTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 2 of 4 (trusted-accepted). A server cert chaining to the client's trusted server CA is accepted (same fn as the happy path).

entries.property: server-cert verification: wrong server identity REJECTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 3 of 4. A trusted-CA server cert presenting the WRONG identity (SAN) is rejected — chain validity alone is not sufficient.

entries.property: server-cert verification: expired server cert REJECTED
entries.notes: Server-auth case 4 of 4. A server cert past its validity window is rejected.

entries.property: server-cert verification: empty server CA fails closed (no implicit trust)
entries.notes: Defense-in-depth for the four server-auth cases: an empty server trust anchor set does not silently accept any server — it fails closed.

entries.property: env key source loads + signs (MCPS-076): dev-feature build reads the seed and signs correctly WITHOUT mutating the process environment (issue #25)
entries.notes: MCPS-076 (audit gap G-3) + issue #25: the dev/CI-only EnvKeySource (feature dev_env_key_source) loads all material and signs. The read is now a PURE read — it does NOT remove the seed env var (the prior std::env::remove_var was unsound under threads); child-process secret isolation is the inner-launch env policy's job (no env inheritance by default), not a global mutation here. This is the positive control for the env-keysource posture, in a feature-gated unit test rather than the production binary.

entries.property: key-source errors never leak secret material
entries.notes: Defense-in-depth for the env-keysource path: error messages from the key source never echo the secret seed.

entries.property: audit-evidence vocabulary drift (ADR-MCPS-035): every emitted rejection reason is a member of the frozen McpReError::wire_code() taxonomy and the success-event set is exactly the two-item allowlist
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-036 gate item (c) / issue #151. The audit vocabulary (mcp-re-core/src/audit.rs) mints no rejection reason outside the frozen wire_code() taxonomy (error.rs is the sole authority), read from disk via runfiles. Companion assertions in the same target keep the success allowlist exact (success_event_set_is_exactly_the_two_item_allowlist) and forbid any authorization_hash_mismatch notion (no_authorization_hash_mismatch_audit_reason).

entries.property: forbidden-claim drift (ADR-MCPS-036): no forbidden over-claim wording appears as an asserted live claim in the proposal-facing docs
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-036 gate item (d) / issue #155. The forbidden-wording list (co-located with §A's Forbidden-claim column) does not appear as a live claim in security-boundary / v0.5-claim-matrix / threat-coverage / composability / proposal-scope, read from disk via runfiles. A phrase inside a Forbidden-claim table cell or a negated sentence is legitimately naming the non-goal and is allowed.

entries.property: cloud-KMS seam: a KMS-produced Ed25519 response signature verifies under the mcp-re-core verifier (provider-agnostic delegation)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. ADR-MCPS-028 provider-agnostic KMS response-signer core (the shared seam behind the #3838 delegation). Positive control: a signature from the generic KMS wrapper (mock backend) verifies under the same mcp-re-core VerificationKey path production uses. Runs under the default-feature proxy_unit_test (the seam is always compiled, not feature-gated).

entries.property: cloud-KMS seam fails closed: a backend returning a non-64-byte signature is rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. A KMS backend that returns a wrong-length (non-Ed25519) signature is rejected at the seam before it can be emitted — malformed KMS output never reaches the wire.

entries.property: cloud-KMS seam fails closed: a backend reporting a non-Ed25519 public key is rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. The seam refuses a backend whose reported SPKI public key is not Ed25519 — the response-signing identity is pinned to Ed25519 (no algorithm substitution).

entries.property: cloud-KMS seam fails closed: prehash-mode signing (KMS signs a digest, not the raw preimage) is caught
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. Ed25519ph (prehashed) is a DIFFERENT primitive from pure Ed25519; a backend that prehashes would produce a signature the Core verifier rejects. The seam catches the prehash/pure mismatch so a misconfigured KMS key can never silently downgrade the signing primitive.

entries.property: cloud-KMS seam fails closed: a signature not matching the reported public key is rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. A signature produced by a key that does not correspond to the backend's reported public key fails closed at the seam — the emitted signature is always self-consistent with the advertised signer identity.

entries.property: GCP Cloud KMS backend: signs an Ed25519 response that verifies end-to-end under mcp-re-core (offline, local-seed backend)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. ADR-MCPS-028 §C native GCP Cloud KMS Ed25519 response signer. Offline positive control (for_test_with_local_seed, no network) exercised under the both-KMS-features proxy_ext_unit_test: a GCP-backed signature verifies under the production mcp-re-core path. The #[ignore]d gcp_kms_live_test is the online complement (manual live-infra lane).

entries.property: GCP Cloud KMS backend fails closed: a non-Ed25519 key algorithm is rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. The GCP adapter refuses a key version whose algorithm is not Ed25519 (EC_SIGN_ED25519) — no algorithm substitution from the cloud KMS side.

entries.property: GCP Cloud KMS backend fails closed: a prehash signature is rejected before return
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. Defense-in-depth for the seam's prehash guard, specialized to the GCP backend: a prehashed signature is caught before the adapter returns it.

entries.property: GCP Cloud KMS backend: the reported public key (PEM) parses to a well-formed Ed25519 SPKI (identity binding)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. The public key GCP reports (PEM-encoded) parses to the exact Ed25519 SPKI the verifier binds — the response-signer identity advertised to clients is the one KMS holds.

entries.property: GCP Cloud KMS delegated TLS: the handshake signature verifies under the reported SPKI (non-exporting device signer)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. ADR-MCPS-028 §G delegated TLS handshake signing fronting the GCP KMS key: the TLS-layer signature verifies under the same reported SPKI, so the device/KMS never exports the private key yet still terminates the handshake.

entries.property: AWS KMS backend: signs an Ed25519 response that verifies end-to-end under mcp-re-core (offline, local-seed backend)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. ADR-MCPS-028 §B native AWS KMS Ed25519 response signer (minimal audited SigV4, no async aws-sdk-kms). Offline positive control (for_test_with_local_seed, no network) under proxy_ext_unit_test: an AWS-backed signature verifies under the production mcp-re-core path. The #[ignore]d aws_kms_live_test is the online complement.

entries.property: AWS KMS backend fails closed: a non-Ed25519 key spec is rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. The AWS adapter refuses a KMS key whose spec is not Ed25519 — no algorithm substitution from the cloud KMS side.

entries.property: AWS KMS backend fails closed: a prehash signature is rejected before return
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. Defense-in-depth for the seam's prehash guard, specialized to the AWS backend: a prehashed signature is caught before the adapter returns it.

entries.property: AWS KMS backend: the reported public key (SPKI DER) parses to a well-formed Ed25519 key (identity binding)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. The public key AWS reports (SPKI DER) parses to the exact Ed25519 key the verifier binds — the advertised response-signer identity is the one KMS holds.

entries.property: AWS KMS delegated TLS: the handshake signature verifies under the reported SPKI (non-exporting device signer)
entries.notes: MCPS-72 (#252) / MCPS-56. ADR-MCPS-028 §G delegated TLS handshake signing fronting the AWS KMS key: the TLS-layer signature verifies under the same reported SPKI, so the device/KMS never exports the private key yet still terminates the handshake.

entries.property: Mode C attested ingress refuses to start without the explicit pinned-mTLS acknowledgement (fail closed)
entries.notes: MCPS-62/MCPS-61. ADR-MCPS-023 §C2 / §Conformance. --transport-binding attested-ingress without --ingress-pinned-mtls is rejected at parse time; the pinned attestor→node channel is load-bearing and cannot be silently absent. GREEN-OFFLINE.

entries.property: Mode B demotion: plain lb-assertion (Tier 3) transport binding is strict-rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-62. ADR-MCPS-023 §B1. The plain lb-assertion path places the LB in the TCB without the Mode-C strict bar; strict/production refuses to enable it (legacy/migration only, never presented beside Mode A/C). GREEN-OFFLINE.

entries.property: Mode B demotion: the Tier-2 trusted reverse-proxy identity header is strict-rejected
entries.notes: MCPS-62. ADR-MCPS-023 §B1. The forwarded trusted-ingress identity header (Tier 2) is spoofable by any peer that can reach the socket; strict refuses it. Raw forwarded-identity headers remain forbidden under strict. GREEN-OFFLINE.

entries.property: method-transparency: the verify verdict is independent of the JSON-RPC method
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-030/034 behavioral half; §A Tool safety

entries.property: no concrete MCP method-name literal in non-test Core source
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-034 static half; §A Tool safety

entries.property: RFC 9421 request signature verified; a tampered request body is rejected
entries.notes: §A Message authenticity

entries.property: any mutation of the signed payload (body or JSON-RPC id) is rejected
entries.notes: §A Integrity

entries.property: the request is bound to its declared audience; a wrong-audience request is rejected
entries.notes: §A Audience binding

entries.property: authorization binding: the client binds an authorization artifact into the RFC 9421 request evidence
entries.notes: §A Delegation / authorization binding

entries.property: freshness: an expired request is rejected
entries.notes: §A Freshness

entries.property: replay: a second submission of the same nonce is rejected
entries.notes: §A Replay resistance

entries.property: trust resolution: a signer key not in the authorized set fails closed
entries.notes: §A Revocation / trust propagation

entries.property: transport binding: the verified request actor is the identity the transport binding checks the channel against
entries.notes: §A Ingress binding

entries.property: key admission: a keyid outside the admitted set is rejected
entries.notes: §A Key custody / blast radius

entries.property: response bound to its originating request; a response bound to the WRONG request is rejected
entries.notes: §A Response binding

entries.property: the request evidence block is client-authored and covered by content-digest; a caller-injected _meta not covered by the signature is rejected
entries.notes: §A Verified security context

entries.property: delegated-required serving: a delegated-signed SUCCESS response verifies via the credential→root attestation chain and the root issuer is touched only at issuance (never per request)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §4/§5 hot path (MCPRE-122). root_invocations()==1 across 5 served responses — the KMS/root stays off the request path.

entries.property: delegated-required serving: a request-BOUND rejection receipt is delegated-signed and verifies via the bound delegated path (mcp-re.replay_detected)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §4. A verified request's later replay is refused with a request-bound, delegated-signed receipt.

entries.property: delegated-required serving: a PREFLIGHT (unbound) rejection is delegated-signed and honestly classified unbound — not falsely request-bound
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §4. A never-verified request yields an unbound delegated rejection; the bound path must NOT accept it as request-bound.

entries.property: delegated-required serving: no active delegated key FAILS CLOSED — 503 + frozen mcp-re.delegated_signing_unavailable + unsigned (no direct-root sign, no unsigned 200)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §6 availability floor. The serving-side no-key fault maps to the frozen delegated_signing_unavailable token and is emitted UNSIGNED.

entries.property: delegated-required serving: a DIRECT-root (pre-052) response is rejected mcp-re.delegation_credential_missing — no downgrade fallback
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §3. A root-signed response carrying no inline credential is refused by a delegated-required verifier.

entries.property: delegated-required production wiring: real config serves & verifies, ROTATES K1→K2 (successor verifies; root touched only at rotation), and FAILS CLOSED past successor expiry — no stale-key extension, no direct-root fallback
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §4/§6 through the real build_delegated_signing + HttpProfileProxy::new_delegated. Rotation success + fail-closed in one production-wired omnibus.

entries.property: delegated-required E2E: a plain MCP client → client-proxy → server-proxy → backend round-trips to a client-verified delegated success and back to plain MCP
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 two-proxy altitude. Real ClientProxy (DelegatedRequired) ↔ real HttpProfileProxy, no MCP-RE fields leaking to the plain client.

entries.property: delegated-required E2E: a replay yields a request-bound delegated rejection the client verifies and classifies mcp-re.replay_detected (fail closed, no result)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052. The return-leg rejection is verified request-bound end to end and converted to a plain JSON-RPC error.

entries.property: delegated-required E2E: the client REFUSES a delegated success signed by a REVOKED delegated key (mcp-re.delegation_revoked) — the revocation seam is live end to end
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §7. The client StaticRevocationList names the server's delegated_kid; an otherwise-valid success fails closed.

entries.property: delegated-required E2E: a non-empty revocation source that does NOT name the server key still round-trips — the seam answers, it does not blanket-deny
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §7. Proves the revocation predicate is a real allow/deny decision, not fail-everything.

entries.property: delegated credential-verification matrix: the frozen d01–d22 corpus verifies black-box through the one production entry point to its exact expected mcp-re.* verdict
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §9 executable spec. Every credential-verification failure mode (expired/nbf, key_use, profile, audience, trust-epoch, revoked, key-mismatch, issuer-untrusted, stripped/lifted credential, body/signature tamper) is a frozen KAT.

entries.property: delegated credential-verification matrix: the corpus is ≥22 vectors and every frozen delegation wire token appears at least once (taxonomy coverage)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §9. Guards the frozen delegation token SET against silent narrowing; Display/wire_code stability is additionally asserted by mcp-re-core delegation_wire_strings.

entries.property: trust-anchor rotation: during a root overlap window BOTH the outgoing and incoming roots are accepted
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 master/root key rotation (trust-anchor rotation, NOT delegated-key rotation). TrustedIssuerSet: Root A retired-in-window + Root B current ⇒ credentials under either verify. Matrix §H.

entries.property: trust-anchor rotation: after the overlap window closes the retired root is rejected and only the new root is accepted
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 root rotation cutover. A retired root past its valid_until resolves untrusted (delegation_issuer_untrusted) even though the credential's own exp has not passed; the incoming root verifies. Matrix §H.

entries.property: trust-anchor: an issuer absent from the trusted-root set is rejected (unknown root)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052. An issuer_kid not in current/retired resolves to None ⇒ delegation_issuer_untrusted. Matrix §H.

entries.property: root revocation: revoking an issuer_kid invalidates EVERY descendant delegated credential immediately, before its own exp
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 root/master-key revocation. One decisive action (revoke the issuer) ⇒ delegation_revoked for all its delegated credentials, without chasing each delegated key and before the credential exp. Matrix §H.

entries.property: root revocation isolation: revoking one root leaves other trusted roots fully accepted
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052. Revoking Root A rejects its credentials (delegation_revoked) while Root B still verifies. Matrix §H.

entries.property: root issuance failure: an unavailable KMS root serves only until the current delegated key expires, then fails closed (never extends, never falls back to direct-root)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §6 server-side master-key-outage contract at the rotor altitude: K1 mints, successor issuance fails (root down), K1 kept while valid, fails closed at K1 exp, CustodyError::FailClosedIssuance thereafter — no stale extension, no direct-root fallback. Matrix §H.

entries.property: root rotation via a SIGNED trust-anchor manifest with an auto-provisioned root — no human-in-the-loop key creation; A→A+B overlap→B, then A revoked, plus manifest rollback protection
entries.notes: ADR-MCPRE-052 §I. A TestRootAuthorityProvider mints Root B on the fly; an org-signed TrustAnchorManifest (current/retiring-valid_until/revoked, versioned, expiring) carries the rotation; credentials verify/reject per manifest and a rollback to the pre-revocation version is refused. Hermetic twin of the live Cloud KMS lane (gcp_kms_root_rotation_live, doc-cited §I).

entries.property: MRT continuation: a multi-round-trip continuation opened on replica A is honoured on replica B (stateless cross-replica, shared correlation tier)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-047. Open leg on A records the retained bases in the shared continuation store; the answer leg on B (different signer + replay tier) recovers them and binds the continuation, then serves a terminal reply. The live analogue is Proof 3 of docs/security/gke-multi-replica-validation.sh.

entries.property: MRT continuation: an answer leg with no shared-store entry fails closed (continuation_binding_failed)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-047. No retained bases (never opened / expired / one-shot already consumed) => the pure dispatcher rejects the binding; a replayed or forged continuation never admits.

entries.property: MRT continuation: a tampered requestState breaks the binding (continuation_binding_failed)
entries.notes: ADR-MCPS-047. The wire requestState is keyed into the shared store and digest-bound under the client's signature; a mismatch fails closed.

counts.comment: Recorded count. The guard re-derives this from the entries array length and FAILS on mismatch.
